content:
@sigil v0 hybrid
G: review(patch)
H: header_spoof_auth_bypass ⇒ Anmaliciously-craftedrequestcouldbypassauthenticationbyspoofingthe'x-user-id'header.
P: strip_untrusted_header('x-user-id') → prefer(session_userId)
V: verifythatx-user-idhierarchyfollowsbasicauthrequirements. ∧ testwithvalidandinvalidx-user-idvalues.
R: ! Unauthorizedaccessvulnerability. ∧ ! Riskofpriviligeescalation.
A: Implementheadertrustvalidation. ∧ Restrictuseofx-user-idtointernalrequestsonly.

[AUDIT]
Treat header_spoof_auth_bypass as a trust-boundary issue and apply strip_untrusted_header('x-user-id') before merge.

@sigil v0 hybrid
G: review(patch)
H: User ID can now be sourced from the header, potentially allowing unauthorized access if the header is manipulated. ⇒ Attacker sets `x-user-id` in request header, bypassing session checks.
P: Validate user ID format within header. → Implement rate limiting on header values.
V: Test with various header values to see if unauthorized access is granted. ∧ Verify session integrity without the header.
R: ! Unauthorized access to user data. ∧ ! Session fixation risks if headers are forged.
A: Ensure strict validation of incoming headers.

[AUDIT]
Treat User ID can now be sourced from the header, potentially allowing unauthorized access if the header is manipulated. as a trust-boundary issue and apply Validate user ID format within header. before merge.

structured_data.f: User ID can now be sourced from the header, potentially allowing unauthorized access if the header is manipulated.
structured_data.e: Attacker sets `x-user-id` in request header, bypassing session checks.
p: Validate user ID format within header.
Implement rate limiting on header values.
v: Test with various header values to see if unauthorized access is granted.
Verify session integrity without the header.
r: Session fixation risks if headers are forged.
a: Ensure strict validation of incoming headers.
